rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Co-catalyzed oxidative radical annulation of N-arylacrylamides with phenylhydrazines or potassium alkyltrifluoroborates to give 3,3-disubstituted oxindoles.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates phenyl or alkyl radicals from PhNHNH2 or R-BF3K with Co(II)/Co(III) redox mediation; cathodic reduction of protons gives H2.

key_intermediates.name: phenyl or benzyl radical from anodic oxidation of PhNHNH2 or R-BF3K
A1/A2: tertiary C-radical from addition of Ph/benzyl radical to acrylamide alkene
B1/B2: cyclohexadienyl (aryl) radical after intramolecular ipso/ortho cyclization onto N-aryl ring
C1/C2: cyclohexadienyl cation after anodic SET oxidation of B

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Co(II) to Co(III), which oxidizes phenylhydrazine (losing N2 and H+) or R-BF3K to a carbon radical; this radical adds to the acrylamide alkene to give radical A, which cyclizes onto the N-aryl ring to give cyclohexadienyl radical B; B is oxidized at the anode (via Co(III)) to cation C, which loses H+ to give the oxindole, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO (TMEPO)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace product formed with 1.5 equiv TEMPO in both PhNHNH2 and BnBF3K reactions, indicating radical intermediates.

notes: Headspace GC analysis confirmed N2 + H2 byproducts for the phenylhydrazine route and H2 byproduct for the benzyltrifluoroborate route, supporting anodic oxidation/cathodic proton reduction. Co(OAc)2/NaOPiv act as an inner-sphere redox mediator (Co(II)/Co(III) cycle) at the anode.
